title: Which Antigravity surface should I use? 2.0 vs CLI vs IDE vs SDK
summary: Google Antigravity is an agent-first software development platform that helps developers build, orchestrate, and monitor AI agents. It offers multiple interfaces including a desktop app (2.0), CLI, IDE, and SDK for flexible development workflows.
writeup: This video compares the four surfaces of Google Antigravity, an agent-first software development platform for building, orchestrating, and monitoring AI agents: the 2.0 desktop app, the CLI, the IDE, and the SDK. It frames the choice as a matter of fit, mapping each interface to different development workflows. The takeaway is that Antigravity supports multi-agent systems and code generation across multiple entry points, letting developers pick the surface that matches the task at hand.
